jokes.text: The big new accessory with eyeglasses...seems to be that strap that connects so you can take them on and off...which I don't get because if you have glasses, isn't that because you need to wear glasses?
jokes.setup: Jerry observes the popularity of eyeglass straps as an accessory
jokes.punchline: The logical contradiction - if you need glasses, why would you want to take them off?
jokes.explanation: Classic Jerry observational humor with solid logical contradiction premise

jokes.text: People with crutches don't have a chain attached to their belt...so they can just let go of them every now and then.
jokes.setup: Jerry extends the eyeglass strap logic to other medical aids
jokes.punchline: The absurd image of people casually dropping their crutches
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of the premise with vivid absurd imagery. Economy is excellent.

jokes.text: Why not get a toupee with a rubber band for water-skiing?
jokes.setup: Jerry continues the detachable accessory theme
jokes.punchline: Absurd image of a toupee bouncing around while water-skiing
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and visual absurdity. Sets up toupee theme for later payoffs.

jokes.text: All right. Tell me. What's kept you so busy? / Mostly chemotherapy. / Hey, I'll see you.
jokes.setup: George aggressively demands explanation for Gary's absence
jokes.punchline: Gary's matter-of-fact delivery of devastating news, followed by George's instant retreat
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy - George's self-absorption meeting uncomfortable reality. Exceptional economy and timing.

jokes.text: Hey, pig!
jokes.setup: Kramer yells what appears to be an insult
jokes.punchline: Revealed later he was yelling at a litterbug, not the cop
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection setup that pays off with explanation. Very economical.

jokes.text: So, didn't you explain that to the cop? / No, I fled the scene.
jokes.setup: Jerry assumes Kramer would handle the misunderstanding rationally
jokes.punchline: Kramer's completely irrational response to flee instead of explain
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer logic - choosing the most dramatic option over the sensible one.

jokes.text: - You gonna share with me next time? / - Sure. I swear. / - All right. I'm looking forward to it.
jokes.setup: Kramer physically forces George to promise to share secrets
jokes.punchline: Kramer's earnest anticipation of George's future secrets
jokes.explanation: Solid character moment showing Kramer's childlike insistence on fairness.

jokes.text: Why did he tell you and not me? / - I don't know. / - How are you closer to him than me?
jokes.setup: George learns Jerry knew about Gary's cancer
jokes.punchline: George's petty jealousy over being excluded from cancer news
jokes.explanation: Pure George - making someone else's illness about himself and his social standing.

jokes.text: Really? How bad? Was he on his deathbed? / No, he was on his regular bed.
jokes.setup: George asks dramatically about Gary's condition
jokes.punchline: Jerry's literal, deflating response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry deadpan delivery. Excellent wordplay with maximum economy.

jokes.text: It's not like you're my wife.
jokes.setup: George expects Jerry to tell him everything
jokes.punchline: Jerry's dismissive comparison that becomes a running theme
jokes.explanation: Sets up running gag that pays off multiple times. Simple but effective.

jokes.text: I was so nice to him I almost made myself sick.
jokes.setup: Jerry explains why George was better off not knowing about the cancer
jokes.punchline: Jerry's forced niceness became physically uncomfortable
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jerry - his shallow nature making even genuine kindness uncomfortable.

jokes.text: That's right. You let him have it. / Who is he not to tell you about his life-threatening illness? / That's what I'm saying. / His illness is your business. / If not mine, whose? / If not now, when?
jokes.setup: Jerry sarcastically encourages George's outrage
jokes.punchline: Escalating to absurd philosophical justifications for being told about cancer
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation with Jerry feeding George's narcissism. The final philosophical questions are perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: You told her? She's not your wife.
jokes.setup: George discovers Elaine knows about Gary's cancer
jokes.punchline: George uses Jerry's own phrase against him
jokes.explanation: Good callback structure, showing George learning Jerry's dismissive tactics.

jokes.text: - You don't trust my poker face? / - Do you ever win at poker? / No.
jokes.setup: George defends his ability to keep secrets
jokes.punchline: Jerry's logical question immediately deflates George's argument
jokes.explanation: Perfect logical trap by Jerry with excellent economy. George's honest 'No' is perfectly timed.

jokes.text: That's like me buying a wheelchair to cruise around in. / Yeah, I've considered that.
jokes.setup: Elaine criticizes Kramer wanting glasses as a fashion accessory
jokes.punchline: Kramer's matter-of-fact admission he's considered the wheelchair idea
jokes.explanation: Exceptional Kramer moment - his casual consideration of exploiting disability for convenience is perfectly in character and shocking.

jokes.text: Yes, I do, but I don't want anyone else to have them. / Well, that's peculiar.
jokes.setup: Jake admits he knows where to get the glasses but won't share
jokes.punchline: Kramer's understated response to Jake's obvious pettiness
jokes.explanation: Good character revelation of Jake's petty exclusivity, with Kramer's typical understated reaction.

jokes.text: You know, you'd get two pair, the whole table knows.
jokes.setup: Gary explains why he didn't tell George about his cancer
jokes.punchline: Using poker terminology to describe George's inability to keep secrets
jokes.explanation: Clever callback using poker metaphor to explain George's transparency.

jokes.text: In 1979, I ticketed a brown Dodge Diplomat for parking in a church zone. That fine was never paid. And since then, that scofflaw has piled up more parking tickets than anyone in New York City.
jokes.setup: Officer explains his long pursuit of a parking scofflaw
jokes.punchline: The epic nature of his obsession with parking tickets over 16 years
jokes.explanation: Good character establishment of the officer's obsessive nature and the absurd stakes of parking enforcement.

jokes.text: he's become my white whale.
jokes.setup: Officer's obsession with the parking scofflaw
jokes.punchline: Comparing parking enforcement to Captain Ahab's obsession with Moby Dick
jokes.explanation: Brilliant literary reference that elevates mundane parking enforcement to epic literature. Perfect economy and highly quotable.

jokes.text: I like that eye patch.
jokes.setup: Serious conversation about law enforcement
jokes.punchline: Kramer's complete distraction by the officer's eye patch
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kramer moment - completely missing the point of serious conversation to focus on fashion.

jokes.text: What do you think, I tell Jerry everything? It's not like he's my wife.
jokes.setup: George asks Kramer not to tell Jerry a secret
jokes.punchline: Kramer uses the same phrase Jerry used earlier
jokes.explanation: Running gag continuation showing how phrases spread through the group.

jokes.text: Well, I've been living a lie. / Just one? I'm living, like, 20.
jokes.setup: George dramatically confesses to living a lie
jokes.punchline: Kramer's casual one-upmanship about his multiple lies
jokes.explanation: Excellent deflation of George's drama with Kramer's casual confession. Perfect character work and timing.

jokes.text: Well, I never actually had cancer. / I'll see you.
jokes.setup: George's big confession builds dramatic tension
jokes.punchline: The shocking revelation followed by Kramer's immediate exit
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark revelation that recontextualizes the entire episode. Kramer's exit mirrors George's earlier reaction to real cancer news.

jokes.text: I never said hi. When did I say hi? / - I never heard her say hi.
jokes.setup: Elaine learns Kramer told Jake she said hi
jokes.punchline: Elaine's frantic denial and Jerry's unhelpful confirmation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Elaine's obsession with relationship protocol and Jerry's literal-mindedness.

jokes.text: I had the upper hand in the post-breakup relationship. It's like a game of tag.
jokes.setup: Elaine explains the importance of who made last contact
jokes.punchline: Comparing complex relationship dynamics to a children's game
jokes.explanation: Excellent analogy reducing adult relationship games to childhood simplicity. Very quotable concept.

jokes.text: So that's your poker face.
jokes.setup: George clearly has something on his mind but denies it
jokes.punchline: Jerry immediately identifies George's obvious tell
jokes.explanation: Good callback that proves Jerry's earlier point about George's inability to hide anything.

jokes.text: What are you talking about? / Two pair? Three of a kind? / Will you stop it? / Oh, my God. You got a flush. / You're holding a flush. / I don't have a flush. / A full house? You got a full house?
jokes.setup: Jerry reads George's obvious tells
jokes.punchline: Jerry's increasingly excited poker hand guessing
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the poker metaphor with Jerry getting increasingly excited about 'reading' George.

jokes.text: What do you got? / Gary Fogel never had cancer!
jokes.setup: Jerry demands to see George's 'cards'
jokes.punchline: George 'reveals his hand' with the shocking cancer lie revelation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant structural payoff where the poker metaphor culminates in the episode's biggest revelation. Perfect timing and escalation.

jokes.text: You still like me, don't you? / Correct. What?
jokes.setup: Jake logically follows Elaine's denials
jokes.punchline: Elaine accidentally confirms her feelings then immediately realizes her mistake
jokes.explanation: Good logical trap that catches Elaine in her own game-playing, with perfect timing on her realization.

jokes.text: Coming down to say that you didn't say hi is more of a gesture than if you did say hi.
jokes.setup: Elaine tries to maintain distance by denying she said hi
jokes.punchline: Jake points out the logical flaw in her strategy
jokes.explanation: Clever observation about relationship psychology - the meta-communication being stronger than the original message.

jokes.text: There's one other person who might do something like this, and that's you. / Well... / I don't even think you could do it. / No, I could do it.
jokes.setup: Jerry compares Gary's cancer lie to George's character
jokes.punchline: George's defensive insistence that he could fake cancer too
jokes.explanation: Perfect George moment - being insulted that he's not considered capable of a truly despicable act.

jokes.text: I bought him an unlimited gift certificate at the Hair Team For Men just to put his mind at ease?
jokes.setup: Jerry explains his kind gesture for Gary's supposed chemo hair loss
jokes.punchline: The absurd generosity for a completely fake problem
jokes.explanation: Good irony of Jerry's rare generosity being completely wasted on a lie. The specific gift choice is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: So because you're getting free parking, I gotta pretend this guy had cancer? / Yeah.
jokes.setup: Jerry protests the moral compromise
jokes.punchline: George's matter-of-fact confirmation of the trade-off
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of George's moral flexibility - reducing a cancer deception to a parking arrangement.

jokes.text: I'm getting rid of all my fillings because that mercury's toxic. / Hey, let me see your fillings.
jokes.setup: Gary mentions his dental health concerns
jokes.punchline: Jerry's sudden inappropriate interest in examining Gary's mouth
jokes.explanation: Mildly funny moment of Jerry's awkward attempt to verify Gary's lies through dental inspection.

jokes.text: I wanna be a pirate.
jokes.setup: Officer is wearing an eye patch
jokes.punchline: Kramer's childlike desire inspired by the pirate aesthetic
jokes.explanation: Pure Kramer - immediate fascination with a romantic fantasy inspired by a simple accessory.

jokes.text: Can't see on your right side? / No. It's itchy.
jokes.setup: Kramer asks about the obvious problem with an eye patch
jokes.punchline: Officer's completely mundane complaint instead
jokes.explanation: Good deflation of expectations - ignoring the obvious serious problem for a trivial annoyance.

jokes.text: Once belonged to Jon Voight.
jokes.setup: Kramer shows off his car
jokes.punchline: Claiming celebrity ownership with typical Kramer confidence
jokes.explanation: Classic Kramer grandiose claim with no verification. Sets up potential story thread.

jokes.text: I didn't say hi. / You didn't? / No, I told him to send you my regards. I didn't say hi. / Regards?
jokes.setup: Elaine tries to clarify what she actually said
jokes.punchline: The absurd distinction between 'hi' and 'regards' and Jake's confusion
jokes.explanation: Good exploration of the absurd precision of relationship communication protocols.

jokes.text: Good. Revenge is very good.
jokes.setup: Elaine mentions getting back at Jake
jokes.punchline: Kramer's enthusiastic endorsement of revenge
jokes.explanation: Good Kramer moment of enthusiastic support for questionable behavior.

jokes.text: Malaysia.
jokes.setup: Elaine asks where he got his glasses
jokes.punchline: The completely unexpected and exotic location
jokes.explanation: Perfect unexpected answer that explains Jake's exclusivity - they're literally from across the world.

jokes.text: Let's start the bidding.
jokes.setup: Elaine wants to buy his glasses
jokes.punchline: The man treats it like an auction
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation showing New York opportunism - everything becomes a business transaction.

jokes.text: I never had feelings for Gary until he got sick. But he was so brave and gained such a wonderful perspective on life. I fell in love with him.
jokes.setup: Woman explains her attraction to Gary
jokes.punchline: The dramatic irony that she fell for his fake bravery about fake cancer
jokes.explanation: Strong dramatic irony - the audience knows her romantic feelings are based on a complete lie.

jokes.text: Well, in the old days when the senators didn't vote the way that the party leaders wanted them to, they whipped them. 'You better vote the way we want you to, or there's gonna be big trouble.'
jokes.setup: Kramer explains political terminology
jokes.punchline: His literal interpretation of the 'whip' position
jokes.explanation: Mild Kramer misunderstanding of political processes. Not particularly strong.

jokes.text: Well, she's talking a blue streak now, Jack.
jokes.setup: Earlier establishment that the woman never talks to anyone
jokes.punchline: Kramer's observation that she's now very talkative with Gary
jokes.explanation: Good callback observation with nice period slang ('blue streak', 'Jack').

jokes.text: Newman? The white whale.
jokes.setup: Officer sees the scofflaw who has eluded him
jokes.punchline: Revealing Newman as the epic parking ticket scofflaw
jokes.explanation: Excellent payoff revealing Newman as the legendary parking scofflaw. Perfect character reveal that recontextualizes earlier buildup.

jokes.text: Can you believe someone would lie about chemotherapy to get a wig?
jokes.setup: Discussion about Gary's lies
jokes.punchline: Kramer's moral outrage while completely missing that Gary actually has no cancer at all
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony with Kramer's moral stance based on incomplete information.

jokes.text: She thinks a guy who lies about an illness so he can get some phony hair has perspective?
jokes.setup: Jerry learns about Debby's attraction to Gary
jokes.punchline: Jerry's summary of the absurd romantic situation
jokes.explanation: Good encapsulation of the absurd romantic irony, though somewhat predictable.

jokes.text: See? You're not the only one who has them. I have them too. / Where did you get those? / Malaysia. I was in the area.
jokes.setup: Elaine shows Jake the identical glasses to break his exclusivity
jokes.punchline: Elaine's casual claim about visiting Malaysia
jokes.explanation: Excellent casual lie delivery. 'I was in the area' about Malaysia is perfectly absurd understatement.

jokes.text: Then at your funeral, the mortician comes out, 'Mrs. Costanza...I thought you might want this.'
jokes.setup: Discussion about keeping a toupee secret for life
jokes.punchline: Jerry imagines the mortician returning the toupee to George's mother
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark comedy imagining the ultimate revelation of vanity. Highly quotable and perfectly morbid Jerry observation.

jokes.text: I really can't say. / No, say. I want you to say. / It's not good, okay? It's not good. You look...stupid.
jokes.setup: George demands Jerry's honest opinion about the toupee
jokes.punchline: Jerry's reluctant brutal honesty
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Jerry's characteristic bluntness overcoming his politeness.

jokes.text: Why not get white shoes, move to Miami Beach, and get the whole thing over with?
jokes.setup: Jerry criticizes George's toupee choice
jokes.punchline: Jerry suggests George complete the stereotypical old man transformation
jokes.explanation: Good cultural stereotype joke about aging and Florida retirement clichés.

jokes.text: You people with hair think you're so superior. You have no idea what it's like. You ever look in the tub and see a fistful of hair? Start your day with that!
jokes.setup: Jerry criticizes the salesman for not being helpful
jokes.punchline: Salesman's passionate defense of bald men and vivid hair loss imagery
jokes.explanation: Strong character moment with vivid imagery. The salesman's emotional investment makes it genuinely funny and uncomfortable.

jokes.text: This judge has to use it for some scofflaw. And you know, you can't fight city hall.
jokes.setup: Gary explains why he can't give George the parking space
jokes.punchline: Using 'scofflaw' to describe someone else while being one himself, plus meaningless cliché
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony with Gary unknowingly describing himself, plus callback to the parking ticket storyline.

jokes.text: Where did you get those glasses? / Where? / Those glasses. / Where did you get those glasses? / Where? / Is this supposed to be some kind of a joke on me? Because it's not very funny. Give me those.
jokes.setup: Jake is doing a book signing press conference
jokes.punchline: Reporter's obsession with the glasses completely derailing the literary event
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how the glasses obsession spreads, culminating in public breakdown at Jake's professional moment.
